item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's really cool, and I personally enjoy it as it felt like the old series 1 SCPs which were not necessarily simple, but not overwhelmingly complicated either. This SCP has a semi-basic concept, which is executed wonderfully.
I must admit, I really want to know more about Saint Torben. I think adding more onto what he could do and what relics he made would be sick, I love the idea of an anomalous saint making spooky sacramentals.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I theorize that perhaps he found a way to spread the Catholic faith without being hamstrung by the Papacy and given the eccentricities, perhaps he found a way to convert people through subconscious means and that subconscious influence ties into the cognitohazardness?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a really good idea, but I think we need to know more about Torben the Strange — it feels wrong that all of this is built around a cognitohazardous legend, but we don't get to know what the legend is. And then the researcher asking about details in the first interview log just… stops caring?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The link from the main SCP list still says "ACCESS DENIED", but apart from that this is a pretty solid SCP. It's a good concept with pretty good execution.
+1

item.title: author post
item.content:encoded:
this is my first scp i've posted to the site, so sorry if it isnt quite up to standards

image source is this guy from the wikimedia unidentified saints

thanks to everyone who helped me improve it, hopefully this revision is up to scratch
